design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore room layouts, create/destroy orange blocks to solve spatial puzzles, acquire keys before countdown timer expires, advance through 50 rooms while managing firepower and avoiding enemy contact
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Countdown timer creates constant pressure; unlimited enemy spawning forces quick decision-making; instant death on contact with enemies demands precision platforming
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Cast spells as a sorcerer (Dana) to manipulate the environment, defeat demons, and recover Solomon's Key to restore light to the world
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid puzzle-action design that rejects pure action difficulty in favor of environmental puzzle-solving. Players must think spatially about block manipulation rather than memorize patterns. Designed as reaction to Lode Runner—adds creation mechanics alongside destruction. Incorporates zodiac constellation theming with hidden items and secret levels that alter the ending, rewarding exploration and mastery

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives available as acquirable items within levels; exact stock unknown
difficulty.checkpointing: Room-based progression; no mid-level checkpoints; must acquire key and reach exit before timer expires to advance
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through optional secret levels and seals; player can unlock hidden Seals of Solomon to access Page of Time, Page of Space, and Princess Room, changing ending. Hidden items reward exploration and mastery. GDV (Game Deviation Value) score system provides weighted composite feedback on performance

progression.upgrade_system: Firepower upgrades available as acquirable items within levels; extra lives as items; bonus point items
progression.unlock_mechanism: Constellation seals unlock secret bonus rooms (one per zodiac group); Seals of Solomon unlock final hidden levels; discovery of hidden items gates access to variant endings
replayability_hooks: 15 secret levels out of 64 total require finding specific seals
Ending varies based on which secret levels are completed
Multiple hidden items incentivize re-exploration of rooms
GDV score system encourages optimization of performance metrics

world_building_techniques: Zodiac constellation theming organizing 48 main levels into 12 groups of 4
Greek mythology visual aesthetic inspired by Jason and the Argonauts
Seal of Solomon symbol throughout levels connects to historical Key of Solomon mythology
Ending variation based on player achievement signals narrative branching without cutscenes

danger_signals: Unlimited enemy spawning creates escalating threat perception
Countdown timer and instant-death-on-contact create high-stakes tension
Visual demon aesthetic signals hostile supernatural forces
environmental_storytelling: Orange block architecture defines safe vs. unsafe spaces
Seal symbols marking progression through zodiac constellations
Locked doors requiring keys create goal clarity
Hidden rooms and items suggest deeper world complexity beyond visible progression
